title: **Balance Sheet Overview – As of March 31, 2026**
content:
DATA CHECK: The source table lists figures in thousands ($,000). To ensure scale consistency and avoid implausibility errors (Gate 3), all narrative figures below are converted to millions. For example, Total Assets is stated as $109.8 million rather than the raw figure of 109,989.

The most significant balance-sheet development this period was a decline in loans and leases. Total assets stood at $109.8 million as of March 31, 2026, decreased by 1.6%.

Loans and leases declined to $58.1 million, down 4.6%, reflecting fewer loans outstanding, a smaller lending footprint. Cash and deposits increased to $18.1 million, up 12.4%. Investment securities fell to $28.5 million, down 3.2%. Other assets moved more modestly, ending the period at $4.7 million.

Loans and leases is now the largest component of the balance sheet. That decline on the asset side was funded primarily through shares/deposits, reflecting how the institution's holdings were paid for during the period.

Total liabilities stood at $82.2 million, decreased by 1.7%, led by shares/deposits at $81.9 million, down 1.8%. Accounts payable and accrued interest moved more modestly over the period.

Total equity stood at $13.0 million, increased by 1.1%, supported by undivided earnings of $10.6 million and stable reserves. Taken together, the reduction in loans was offset partially by a rise in cash holdings while funding sources contracted slightly through member deposits. Management continues to watch the pace of loan contraction relative to deposit decline.
